theme.name: Ultima — The Octave of Virtue
theme.description: A companion to the Ultima Tarot built on the bones of Garriott's design: the eight virtues are not a list but a 3-bit algebra over three Principles — Truth (4), Love (2), Courage (1) — so the eight are exactly the eight corners of the additive colour cube (black to white through R/G/B and C/M/Y). Every Ultima 8-fold hangs off that one value, so this deck makes the value the SUIT (a virtue = a colour) and the OCTAVE the rank: each card is one virtue read through one symbol-system — Place of Justice is Yew, Role of Valor is the Fighter, Companion of Spirituality is Shamino. Where the other decks spread meaning across four axes, this one concentrates it: the suit alone carries virtue, colour, binary principle-subset, and prime/composite character at once. The lunar cycle of Felucca runs across it as weather; the majors are the proper nouns the lattice hangs from.
theme.creator: Mykola Bilokonsky & Isaac Z. Schlueter

humility.name: Humility
humility.description: The virtue of Humility — claiming nothing — the ground from which all virtue springs. Principles: none (000 = 0); its colour is black.

symbol.name: Humility (black)
symbol.description: Three pips for Truth, Love, Courage — filled where Humility holds them (000).

virtue.name: Virtue
virtue.description: The Virtue octave — the virtue itself, named and met head-on.
virtue.question: Read through {suit}, what is its Virtue?

place.name: Place
place.description: The Place octave — the city that houses the virtue, or the dungeon that is its shadow.
place.question: Read through {suit}, what is its Place?

role.name: Role
role.description: The Role octave — the avatar-class that practises the virtue, or the shadow it becomes.
role.question: Read through {suit}, what is its Role?

companion.name: Companion
companion.description: The Companion octave — the party-member who embodies the virtue, present or astray.
companion.question: Read through {suit}, what is its Companion?

word.name: Word
word.description: The Word octave — the mantra that invokes the virtue, or its silence.
word.question: Read through {suit}, what is its Word?

stone.name: Stone
stone.description: The Stone octave — the colour-stone of the Abyss, raised rightly or misused.
stone.question: Read through {suit}, what is its Stone?

reagent.name: Reagent
reagent.description: The Reagent octave — the reagent aligned to the virtue, in white magic or black.
reagent.question: Read through {suit}, what is its Reagent?

shrine.name: Shrine
shrine.description: The Shrine octave — the shrine of the virtue, elevation or desecration.
shrine.question: Read through {suit}, what is its Shrine?

transversal.name: The Lunar Cycle
transversal.description: Felucca, the outer moon, turns through eight phases, and at each a moongate opens to one of the eight cities. Across this lattice the moon is weather and timing — under which phase a card is read, not which virtue it is. It moves at the pace of the heavens, indifferent to the virtue or octave it lights.
transversal.ordering_rationale: The natural lunar order, new moon to waning crescent; suit_stride 3 (coprime to 8) so a virtue is lit by a different phase in each octave, keeping the moon cross-cutting rather than pinned to any city.

new-moon.name: New Moon
new-moon.description: Felucca at New Moon — the moongate it opens leads to Humility.
new-moon.visual_motif: no moonlight; a dark gate; the void before the cycle; the silver weather of New Moon laid over the scene.

waxing-crescent.name: Waxing Crescent
waxing-crescent.description: Felucca at Waxing Crescent — the moongate it opens leads to Valor.
waxing-crescent.visual_motif: a thin silver rim; light beginning; the silver weather of Waxing Crescent laid over the scene.

first-quarter.name: First Quarter
first-quarter.description: Felucca at First Quarter — the moongate it opens leads to Compassion.
first-quarter.visual_motif: half dark, half silver; the gate half-open; the silver weather of First Quarter laid over the scene.

waxing-gibbous.name: Waxing Gibbous
waxing-gibbous.description: Felucca at Waxing Gibbous — the moongate it opens leads to Sacrifice.
waxing-gibbous.visual_motif: swelling silver, nearly full; a rising tide of light; the silver weather of Waxing Gibbous laid over the scene.

full-moon.name: Full Moon
full-moon.description: Felucca at Full Moon — the moongate it opens leads to Honesty.
full-moon.visual_motif: a flood of silver-white; the gate wide; all washed in lunar light; the silver weather of Full Moon laid over the scene.

waning-gibbous.name: Waning Gibbous
waning-gibbous.description: Felucca at Waning Gibbous — the moongate it opens leads to Honor.
waning-gibbous.visual_motif: the light past its peak, beginning to ebb; the silver weather of Waning Gibbous laid over the scene.

last-quarter.name: Last Quarter
last-quarter.description: Felucca at Last Quarter — the moongate it opens leads to Justice.
last-quarter.visual_motif: half-light waning; the gate half-closing; the silver weather of Last Quarter laid over the scene.

waning-crescent.name: Waning Crescent
waning-crescent.description: Felucca at Waning Crescent — the moongate it opens leads to Spirituality.
waning-crescent.visual_motif: the last sliver; the cycle closing toward dark; the silver weather of Waning Crescent laid over the scene.

major_arcana.story: The proper nouns of Britannia — the named ones the lattice of virtues hangs from. The Avatar takes up the quest Lord British set; the Guardian and the three early foes (Mondain, Minax, Exodus) oppose it; the three Shadowlords are the living negations of the three Principles; and the Codex, the Ankh, the moons, the Word, and the Abyss are the furniture of the cosmos in which the eight virtues are practised.
major_arcana.visual_style: Spare and iconic on the graphite ground, each named one rendered as a single luminous emblem; the palette free to use any of the eight virtue-colours as the figure demands, since the named ones stand outside the cube even as they move within it.

symbol.name: The Cube
symbol.description: The eight-cornered colour cube of the virtues.

factorization.gloss: Zero: the empty set of principles, the precondition that holds none yet enables all. Pride is the fall from it.

meaning.upright: Humility — claiming nothing — the ground from which all virtue springs. The Virtue of Humility: Humility (black) seen as virtue.
meaning.inverted: Pride — the virtue overturned into pride. Humility inverted toward Pride.

visuals.detailed_description: Humility, rendered as a black emblem (the colour of Humility) on graphite, under the silver weather of new moon.

factorization.gloss: One: the unit, the operator — Courage, the single act that must come first for any other virtue to be practised at all.

meaning.upright: Valor — facing what must be faced. The Virtue of Valor: Valor (red) seen as virtue.
meaning.inverted: Cowardice — the virtue overturned into cowardice. Valor inverted toward Cowardice.

visuals.detailed_description: Valor, rendered as a red emblem (the colour of Valor) on graphite, under the silver weather of waxing gibbous.

factorization.gloss: Two: the first prime — Love, irreducible, the first real reach of the self beyond itself.

meaning.upright: Compassion — feeling with another. The Virtue of Compassion: Compassion (green) seen as virtue.
meaning.inverted: Hatred — the virtue overturned into hatred. Compassion inverted toward Hatred.

visuals.detailed_description: Compassion, rendered as a green emblem (the colour of Compassion) on graphite, under the silver weather of last quarter.

factorization.gloss: Three: prime — Love and Courage fused (yellow), an irreducible giving that no analysis divides.

meaning.upright: Sacrifice — giving of oneself for others. The Virtue of Sacrifice: Sacrifice (yellow) seen as virtue.
meaning.inverted: Selfishness — the virtue overturned into selfishness. Sacrifice inverted toward Selfishness.

visuals.detailed_description: Sacrifice, rendered as a yellow emblem (the colour of Sacrifice) on graphite, under the silver weather of waxing crescent.

factorization.gloss: Four = two squared: Truth as built structure (blue) — a single principle that nonetheless reads as an edifice, the framework reason stands inside.

meaning.upright: Honesty — truth in word and seeming. The Virtue of Honesty: Honesty (blue) seen as virtue.
meaning.inverted: Deceit — the virtue overturned into deceit. Honesty inverted toward Deceit.

visuals.detailed_description: Honesty, rendered as a blue emblem (the colour of Honesty) on graphite, under the silver weather of full moon.

factorization.gloss: Five: prime — Truth and Courage (magenta), the stand taken in the open and answered for.

meaning.upright: Honor — living by one's word in the open. The Virtue of Honor: Honor (magenta) seen as virtue.
meaning.inverted: Disgrace — the virtue overturned into disgrace. Honor inverted toward Disgrace.

visuals.detailed_description: Honor, rendered as a magenta emblem (the colour of Honor) on graphite, under the silver weather of waning crescent.

factorization.gloss: Six = two times three: Truth+Love by the bits (cyan), yet Compassion x Sacrifice by the factors — the card whose binary and prime parents disagree.

meaning.upright: Justice — to each their due — mercy and law in balance. The Virtue of Justice: Justice (cyan) seen as virtue.
meaning.inverted: Tyranny — the virtue overturned into tyranny. Justice inverted toward Tyranny.

visuals.detailed_description: Justice, rendered as a cyan emblem (the colour of Justice) on graphite, under the silver weather of first quarter.

factorization.gloss: Seven: prime — all three principles together (white), the irreducible union that cannot be assembled from fewer.

meaning.inverted: Worldliness — the virtue overturned into worldliness. Spirituality inverted toward Worldliness.

visuals.detailed_description: Spirituality, rendered as a white emblem (the colour of Spirituality) on graphite, under the silver weather of waning gibbous.

visuals.detailed_description: Shamino, rendered as a white emblem (the colour of Spirituality) on graphite, under the silver weather of new moon.

factorization.gloss: Zero: the empty set of principles, the precondition that holds none yet enables all. Pride is the fall from it.

meaning.upright: the mantra LUM — the word of power intoned at the Shrine of Humility. The Word of Humility: Humility (black) seen as word.
meaning.inverted: the unsaid word — the mantra swallowed — the virtue gone mute. Humility inverted toward Pride.

visuals.detailed_description: the mantra LUM, rendered as a black emblem (the colour of Humility) on graphite, under the silver weather of full moon.

factorization.gloss: One: the unit, the operator — Courage, the single act that must come first for any other virtue to be practised at all.

meaning.upright: the mantra RA — the word of power intoned at the Shrine of Valor. The Word of Valor: Valor (red) seen as word.
meaning.inverted: the unsaid word — the mantra swallowed — the virtue gone mute. Valor inverted toward Cowardice.

visuals.detailed_description: the mantra RA, rendered as a red emblem (the colour of Valor) on graphite, under the silver weather of waning crescent.

factorization.gloss: Two: the first prime — Love, irreducible, the first real reach of the self beyond itself.

meaning.upright: the mantra MU — the word of power intoned at the Shrine of Compassion. The Word of Compassion: Compassion (green) seen as word.
meaning.inverted: the unsaid word — the mantra swallowed — the virtue gone mute. Compassion inverted toward Hatred.

visuals.detailed_description: the mantra MU, rendered as a green emblem (the colour of Compassion) on graphite, under the silver weather of first quarter.

factorization.gloss: Three: prime — Love and Courage fused (yellow), an irreducible giving that no analysis divides.

meaning.upright: the mantra CAH — the word of power intoned at the Shrine of Sacrifice. The Word of Sacrifice: Sacrifice (yellow) seen as word.
meaning.inverted: the unsaid word — the mantra swallowed — the virtue gone mute. Sacrifice inverted toward Selfishness.

visuals.detailed_description: the mantra CAH, rendered as a yellow emblem (the colour of Sacrifice) on graphite, under the silver weather of waning gibbous.

factorization.gloss: Four = two squared: Truth as built structure (blue) — a single principle that nonetheless reads as an edifice, the framework reason stands inside.

meaning.upright: the mantra AHM — the word of power intoned at the Shrine of Honesty. The Word of Honesty: Honesty (blue) seen as word.
meaning.inverted: the unsaid word — the mantra swallowed — the virtue gone mute. Honesty inverted toward Deceit.

visuals.detailed_description: the mantra AHM, rendered as a blue emblem (the colour of Honesty) on graphite, under the silver weather of new moon.

factorization.gloss: Five: prime — Truth and Courage (magenta), the stand taken in the open and answered for.

meaning.upright: the mantra SUMM — the word of power intoned at the Shrine of Honor. The Word of Honor: Honor (magenta) seen as word.
meaning.inverted: the unsaid word — the mantra swallowed — the virtue gone mute. Honor inverted toward Disgrace.

visuals.detailed_description: the mantra SUMM, rendered as a magenta emblem (the colour of Honor) on graphite, under the silver weather of waxing gibbous.

factorization.gloss: Six = two times three: Truth+Love by the bits (cyan), yet Compassion x Sacrifice by the factors — the card whose binary and prime parents disagree.

meaning.upright: the mantra BEH — the word of power intoned at the Shrine of Justice. The Word of Justice: Justice (cyan) seen as word.
meaning.inverted: the unsaid word — the mantra swallowed — the virtue gone mute. Justice inverted toward Tyranny.

visuals.detailed_description: the mantra BEH, rendered as a cyan emblem (the colour of Justice) on graphite, under the silver weather of last quarter.

factorization.gloss: Seven: prime — all three principles together (white), the irreducible union that cannot be assembled from fewer.

meaning.upright: the mantra OM — the word of power intoned at the Shrine of Spirituality. The Word of Spirituality: Spirituality (white) seen as word.
meaning.inverted: the unsaid word — the mantra swallowed — the virtue gone mute. Spirituality inverted toward Worldliness.

visuals.detailed_description: the mantra OM, rendered as a white emblem (the colour of Spirituality) on graphite, under the silver weather of waxing crescent.

factorization.gloss: Zero: the empty set of principles, the precondition that holds none yet enables all. Pride is the fall from it.

meaning.upright: the black stone — the Humility stone of the Abyss, raised to pass its barrier. The Stone of Humility: Humility (black) seen as stone.
meaning.inverted: the black stone misused — the stone turned against the one who carries it. Humility inverted toward Pride.

visuals.detailed_description: the black stone, rendered as a black emblem (the colour of Humility) on graphite, under the silver weather of waning gibbous.

factorization.gloss: Zero: the empty set of principles, the precondition that holds none yet enables all. Pride is the fall from it.

meaning.upright: Nightshade — the reagent of poison and the ego's end. The Reagent of Humility: Humility (black) seen as reagent.
meaning.inverted: Nightshade in black magic — the humbling draught turned to murder. Humility inverted toward Pride.

visuals.detailed_description: Nightshade, rendered as a black emblem (the colour of Humility) on graphite, under the silver weather of last quarter.

factorization.gloss: One: the unit, the operator — Courage, the single act that must come first for any other virtue to be practised at all.

meaning.upright: Sulfurous Ash — the reagent of fire and energy. The Reagent of Valor: Valor (red) seen as reagent.
meaning.inverted: Sulfurous Ash in black magic — valor's fire turned only destructive. Valor inverted toward Cowardice.

visuals.detailed_description: Sulfurous Ash, rendered as a red emblem (the colour of Valor) on graphite, under the silver weather of waxing crescent.

meaning.upright: Ginseng — the reagent of healing. The Reagent of Compassion: Compassion (green) seen as reagent.
meaning.inverted: Ginseng in black magic — the healing herb turned to poison. Compassion inverted toward Hatred.

visuals.detailed_description: Ginseng, rendered as a green emblem (the colour of Compassion) on graphite, under the silver weather of full moon.

meaning.upright: Blood Moss — the reagent of movement and blood. The Reagent of Sacrifice: Sacrifice (yellow) seen as reagent.
meaning.inverted: Blood Moss in black magic — the gift of motion turned to bleeding. Sacrifice inverted toward Selfishness.

visuals.detailed_description: Blood Moss, rendered as a yellow emblem (the colour of Sacrifice) on graphite, under the silver weather of waning crescent.

meaning.upright: Black Pearl — the reagent of clarity and direct force. The Reagent of Honesty: Honesty (blue) seen as reagent.
meaning.inverted: Black Pearl in black magic — truth's reagent bent to harm. Honesty inverted toward Deceit.

visuals.detailed_description: Black Pearl, rendered as a blue emblem (the colour of Honesty) on graphite, under the silver weather of first quarter.

factorization.gloss: Five: prime — Truth and Courage (magenta), the stand taken in the open and answered for.

meaning.upright: Garlic — the reagent of warding and protection. The Reagent of Honor: Honor (magenta) seen as reagent.
meaning.inverted: Garlic in black magic — the ward turned to a curse. Honor inverted toward Disgrace.

visuals.detailed_description: Garlic, rendered as a magenta emblem (the colour of Honor) on graphite, under the silver weather of waning gibbous.

factorization.gloss: Six = two times three: Truth+Love by the bits (cyan), yet Compassion x Sacrifice by the factors — the card whose binary and prime parents disagree.

meaning.upright: Spider Silk — the reagent of binding. The Reagent of Justice: Justice (cyan) seen as reagent.
meaning.inverted: Spider Silk in black magic — the binding turned to a snare. Justice inverted toward Tyranny.

visuals.detailed_description: Spider Silk, rendered as a cyan emblem (the colour of Justice) on graphite, under the silver weather of new moon.

meaning.upright: Mandrake Root — the reagent of deepest power. The Reagent of Spirituality: Spirituality (white) seen as reagent.
meaning.inverted: Mandrake Root in black magic — the deep power turned to ruin. Spirituality inverted toward Worldliness.

visuals.detailed_description: Mandrake Root, rendered as a white emblem (the colour of Spirituality) on graphite, under the silver weather of waxing gibbous.

factorization.gloss: Zero: the empty set of principles, the precondition that holds none yet enables all. Pride is the fall from it.

meaning.upright: the Shrine of Humility — the wilderness shrine where Humility is meditated and the Codex glimpsed. The Shrine of Humility: Humility (black) seen as shrine.
meaning.inverted: the shrine desecrated — meditation failed, the vision withheld. Humility inverted toward Pride.

visuals.detailed_description: the Shrine of Humility, rendered as a black emblem (the colour of Humility) on graphite, under the silver weather of waning crescent.

meaning.inverted: the shrine desecrated — meditation failed, the vision withheld. Valor inverted toward Cowardice.

meaning.inverted: the shrine desecrated — meditation failed, the vision withheld. Spirituality inverted toward Worldliness.

factorization.gloss: The Avatar: number 0 (identity).

meaning.upright: you — the seeker who takes up the quest of the eight virtues, the one the cards are dealt to
meaning.inverted: the seeker who quests for power or fame rather than virtue; the false avatar

visuals.detailed_description: The Avatar as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of new moon.

factorization.gloss: Lord British: number 1 (identity).

meaning.upright: the sovereign who set the quest and keeps the realm; rightful authority in service of virtue
meaning.inverted: authority clung to; the king who rules for himself

visuals.detailed_description: Lord British as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of waxing crescent.

factorization.gloss: The Guardian: number 2 (prime).

meaning.upright: the great adversary, the red titan who would master Britannia; domination, the will to control
meaning.inverted: the adversary within — your own will to power, recognized

visuals.detailed_description: The Guardian as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of first quarter.

factorization.gloss: The Codex of Ultimate Wisdom: number 3 (prime).

meaning.upright: the book at the bottom of the Abyss; truth made whole, the answer to the quest
meaning.inverted: wisdom sought as possession; the word taken without the journey

visuals.detailed_description: The Codex of Ultimate Wisdom as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of waxing gibbous.

factorization.gloss: The Ankh: number 4 (composite).

meaning.upright: the sign of the Avatar; life, virtue made visible, resurrection
meaning.inverted: the symbol worn without the substance; piety as costume

visuals.detailed_description: The Ankh as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of full moon.

factorization.gloss: Trammel: number 5 (prime).

meaning.upright: the inner moon, the steady one; constancy, law, the fixed light to steer by
meaning.inverted: rigidity; constancy become inability to change

visuals.detailed_description: Trammel as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of waning gibbous.

factorization.gloss: Felucca: number 6 (composite).

meaning.upright: the outer moon, the changing one that opens the gates; flux, passage, opportunity
meaning.inverted: change for its own sake; the gate taken to flee rather than to seek

visuals.detailed_description: Felucca as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of last quarter.

factorization.gloss: Mondain: number 7 (prime).

meaning.upright: the first foe, the dark wizard and his Gem of Immortality; the refusal of death, power hoarded
meaning.inverted: the fear of ending that drives one to monstrousness

visuals.detailed_description: Mondain as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of waning crescent.

factorization.gloss: Minax: number 8 (composite).

meaning.upright: the enchantress, Mondain's heir; seduction, the twisting of time and desire
meaning.inverted: manipulation; the past or future used to escape the present

visuals.detailed_description: Minax as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of new moon.

factorization.gloss: Exodus: number 9 (composite).

meaning.upright: neither human nor machine, the daemon-engine; the rule of pure system without soul
meaning.inverted: intelligence without conscience; the machine that forgets it serves life

visuals.detailed_description: Exodus as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of waxing crescent.

factorization.gloss: The Shadowlord of Falsehood: number 10 (composite).

meaning.upright: the negation of Truth; the lie that walks, deceit given a body
meaning.inverted: the small lie you tell yourself; falsehood mistaken for tact

visuals.detailed_description: The Shadowlord of Falsehood as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of first quarter.

factorization.gloss: The Shadowlord of Hatred: number 11 (prime).

meaning.upright: the negation of Love; contempt given a body
meaning.inverted: the hardening of the heart; hatred dressed as discernment

visuals.detailed_description: The Shadowlord of Hatred as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of waxing gibbous.

factorization.gloss: The Shadowlord of Cowardice: number 12 (composite).

meaning.upright: the negation of Courage; flight given a body
meaning.inverted: the retreat that calls itself prudence

visuals.detailed_description: The Shadowlord of Cowardice as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of full moon.

factorization.gloss: The Stygian Abyss: number 13 (prime).

meaning.upright: the final descent; the trial that gathers every virtue at once, the way to the Codex
meaning.inverted: the descent refused, or made for the prize and not the passage

visuals.detailed_description: The Stygian Abyss as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of waning gibbous.

factorization.gloss: The Word of Power: number 14 (composite).

meaning.upright: VERAMOCOR — the eight-syllable word that opens the Abyss; the key earned syllable by syllable
meaning.inverted: the shortcut sought; the door forced without the work

visuals.detailed_description: The Word of Power as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of last quarter.

factorization.gloss: The Black Gate: number 15 (composite).

meaning.upright: the doorway the Guardian would open into the world; the threshold of ruin disguised as gift
meaning.inverted: the tempting offer whose true cost is hidden

visuals.detailed_description: The Black Gate as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of waning crescent.

factorization.gloss: The Time Lord: number 16 (composite).

meaning.upright: the keeper of the streams of time; right timing, the long view
meaning.inverted: fatalism, or the meddling that breaks what it would mend

visuals.detailed_description: The Time Lord as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of new moon.

factorization.gloss: The Bell, Book, and Candle: number 17 (prime).

meaning.upright: the three keys to the Abyss; the gathered means, preparation complete
meaning.inverted: the keys collected, the quest never begun

visuals.detailed_description: The Bell, Book, and Candle as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of waxing crescent.

factorization.gloss: The Fellowship: number 18 (composite).

meaning.upright: the cult of false virtue (Unity, Trust, Worthiness) that fronts for the Guardian; counterfeit goodness, the captured community
meaning.inverted: the comfortable lie of belonging; virtue outsourced to a group

visuals.detailed_description: The Fellowship as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of first quarter.

factorization.gloss: The Quest of the Avatar: number 19 (prime).

meaning.upright: the journey itself — the becoming of a virtuous self, not the reaching of a place
meaning.inverted: the quest mistaken for a checklist; the path walked for the badge

visuals.detailed_description: The Quest of the Avatar as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of waxing gibbous.

factorization.gloss: The Gem of Immortality: number 20 (composite).

meaning.upright: the shattered gem that split Britannia into its mirrored lands; the One refracted into the many
meaning.inverted: grasping the whole and shattering it; immortality bought with the world

visuals.detailed_description: The Gem of Immortality as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of full moon.

factorization.gloss: Britannia: number 21 (composite).

meaning.upright: the realm entire — the eight cities, the two moons, the land made of virtue; the world restored and whole
meaning.inverted: the world taken for granted; wholeness that stops questing

visuals.detailed_description: Britannia as a single luminous emblem on graphite, under the silver weather of waning gibbous.
